meta.generated_by: codebase-grounded knowledge graph (understand-style), hand-curated from the v0.5.0 source tree for the teaching manual

layers.name: User code
layers.blurb: What a learner writes: @skill functions, Memory subclasses, analyzer/validator hooks, and a SOUL.md identity.

layers.name: Protocols & hooks
layers.blurb: The two registered protocol kinds (Skill, Memory), the Analyzer/Validator hook helpers, and the cantus.workflows building blocks.

layers.name: Core runtime
layers.blurb: The bounded agent loop: Agent steps over Actions and Observations, records them in an EventStream, and dispatches through the Registry.

layers.name: Substrate
layers.blurb: Where the model and the outside world plug in: model handles and providers, the serve HTTP layer, channels, the TUI, and the environment profiles.

nodes.label: Skill
nodes.blurb: A callable the agent can invoke. Define it three ways: @skill decorator (recommended), register_skill(fn), or subclass Skill (advanced).

nodes.label: Memory
nodes.blurb: Stateful retrieval. Class-only — there is no @memory decorator because memory needs to hold state. Ships four implementations plus an AutoMemory wrapper that exposes view/create/str_replace/delete skills.

nodes.label: Analyzer (hook)
nodes.blurb: A pre-loop hook helper: read the input, return a structured Result before the agent loop starts. Not a protocol kind.

nodes.label: Validator (hook)
nodes.blurb: A post-output hook helper: check the agent's answer and trigger a retry when it fails. Not a protocol kind.

nodes.label: cantus.workflows
nodes.blurb: Composition building blocks that wire skills and hooks into multi-step flows.

nodes.label: Soul (identity)
nodes.blurb: Loads an agent persona from a six-section SOUL.md (name & role, personality, rules, tools, output format, handoffs).

nodes.label: Agent
nodes.blurb: The bounded loop. step() takes one read-act-observe turn; run() loops until a non-empty FinalAnswer or the iteration cap.

nodes.label: EventStream
nodes.blurb: The append-only trace of every Action and Observation. Errors are wrapped as Observations, never raised out of the loop.

nodes.label: Registry
nodes.blurb: Holds the registered Skills and Memories and dispatches calls by name.

nodes.label: Inspector
nodes.blurb: Read-only view over an EventStream. Inspector(stream).replay() reconstructs the full run; @debug marks a Skill/hook for tracing.

nodes.label: Tool-call grammar
nodes.blurb: Constrains the model's tool-call JSON. Adds minLength:1 to final_answer so a constrained decoder cannot emit an empty answer.

nodes.label: Model layer
nodes.blurb: Tier 1 loads local Gemma on Colab (mount_drive_and_load -> ModelHandle). Tier 2 loads any provider (load_chat_model -> ChatModel).

nodes.label: Model providers (8)
nodes.blurb: Eight prefixes resolved by the factory: openai, anthropic, google, groq, nvidia (OpenAI-compatible), ollama (local), mlx (Apple Silicon, no tool use), omlx (local OpenAI-compatible server).

nodes.label: Adapters
nodes.blurb: Bridges a cantus Skill to/from other SDKs (Anthropic Memory tool, MCP, LangChain, DSPy, HuggingFace, OpenHands). Each SDK is lazily imported.

nodes.label: serve (HTTP)
nodes.blurb: FastAPI app factory that exposes a Registry over HTTP, with auth modes none/bearer/api-key and read-only introspection endpoints.

nodes.label: Channels (4)
nodes.blurb: LINE + Telegram over webhooks (HMAC / secret token), Discord over a Gateway WebSocket plus Ed25519 interactions, Google Chat over Cloud Pub/Sub.

nodes.label: cantus tui
nodes.blurb: A Textual terminal dashboard that polls a running serve instance over HTTP — Dashboard, Skills, Permissions, Dataflow, and Inspector panes.

nodes.label: Environments
nodes.blurb: Environment profiles that decide how the model loads and where I/O goes (Colab Drive, local disk, cloud-only).

nodes.label: cantus CLI
nodes.blurb: Two subcommands: serve (start the FastAPI app from a registry import path) and tui (launch the dashboard against a serve URL).

scenarios.title: Colab agent loop (local Gemma)
scenarios.blurb: A student runs an agent inside a notebook against 4-bit Gemma.

steps.action: Each turn the model emits a tool call constrained by the schema (final_answer minLength:1).
Every Action/Observation is appended, including any ValidationErrorObservation retries.

scenarios.title: Multi-provider cloud model
scenarios.blurb: The same agent code points at a cloud provider instead of local Gemma.

steps.action: The prefix selects the provider adapter; its SDK is lazily imported.
ChatModelAsHandle wraps the ChatModel so the Agent's Tier 1 protocol still applies.
Agent(model=handle).run(query) — the loop is identical to the Colab path.

scenarios.title: Serve + channel inbound (LINE)
scenarios.blurb: A LINE message reaches a deployed agent and a reply goes back.

steps.action: cantus serve --registry-import app:registry --channels ... starts the app.
The FastAPI app mounts the LINE webhook route and validates the HMAC-SHA256 signature.
The registered agent processes the message and produces an answer.
The channel sends the reply back via the LINE reply API.

scenarios.title: Live observation (TUI)
scenarios.blurb: An operator watches a running server from the terminal.

steps.action: cantus tui --url http://127.0.0.1:8765 launches the dashboard.
The TUI polls the serve introspection endpoints on an interval.
Read-only endpoints return skills, sessions, permissions, queues, and desensitized workflow traces.
The Dashboard / Skills / Permissions / Dataflow / Inspector panes render the live state.
